SAP Integration Suite: Supporting a wider range of integration needs

As a critical part of SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite has been a reliable source of integration technology for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. And the introduction of new features is making these connections more streamlined, situation aware, and productive—no matter how complex the landscape.

  • Event-driven architecture
    SAP Integration Suite now supports event-driven architecture to enable real-time processes that scale efficiently across distributed environments. It includes advanced event mesh capabilities that allow organizations to handle peak loads with greater ease and run more responsive and resilient operations.

  • Expanded integration library
    The curated library of integrations now includes ServiceNow and Workday solutions, expanding our portfolio to more than 3,000 connectors. Built and maintained by SAP and our partners, these integrations simplify connectivity to third-party applications—enabling smoother workflows and faster deployment.

  • On-premise solution workload management
    Extending beyond cloud-based connections, SAP Integration Suite supports ground-to-ground processes so companies can connect on-premise systems directly. This functionality provides greater flexibility for organizations with hybrid or fully on-premise environments.

  • API fabric for centralized access
    The API fabric enhances developer productivity by centralizing SAP and third-party APIs in a unified catalog. Developers can now quickly discover, register, and access a broad array of APIs, simplifying integration development and accelerating project timelines.

  • EOIO processing
    To support complex asynchronous integration scenarios, SAP Integration Suite now includes “exactly once in order” (EOIO) processing. This capability helps ensure messages are delivered exactly once and in the correct sequence while reducing data errors and enhancing consistency.

🔖Learn more about SAP Integration Suite's new features.

SAP Business AI: Making autonomous finance a reality

 The introduction of autonomous finance capabilities in SAP BTP is making a finance function that requires minimal human intervention a practical and productive reality. Enabled by the addition of SAP Business AI, this update automates complex financial operations—helping companies run with improved agility and resilience.

Leveraging the advanced AI and machine learning models of SAP Business AI, the platform provides SAP S/4HANA Cloud with tools that automate routine tasks, such as transaction processing and reconciliations. ​Recent benchmarking of SAP customers shows that adopting these capabilities paves the way to 90% more-efficient financial reporting, 7% higher forecast accuracy, and 10 times faster planning cycles.

In addition, the enhanced predictive capabilities and real-time data analytics available in SAP BTP equip SAP S/4HANA Cloud with insights that go beyond traditional finance automation. Organizations can now manage risk, increase transparency, and improve accuracy as the cloud ERP anticipates financial outcomes and adapts proactively to changes in cash flow or market volatility.

🔖Learn more about autonomous finance

SAP Build: Stepping up development with ABAP and generative AI

SAP Build solutions have become critical tools for the fast development of applications and automations with a simplified and streamlined builder interface.  Announced in October 2024, The latest integration of ABAP development tools and generative AI in SAP Build is making that experience even better for developers of all skill levels looking to modernize and extend their ERP capabilities.

By connecting tools such as ABAP, Java, JavaScript, and low-code in a unified environment, SAP Build enables direct development access within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making customization faster and easier. The extensibility wizard allows extensions for fields, logic, and processes while maintaining core system integrity.

Prebuilt content in SAP Build accelerates work across functions, including HR, spend management, and procurement. For example, the Joule copilot boosts efficiency with code completion, workflow automation, and instant help access. Developers can also create custom AI skills with drag-and-drop simplicity.

🔖Learn more the latest in SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA

SAP Datasphere: Making data handling simpler and more reliable

SAP Datasphere focused on improving access to mission-critical business data across any cloud infrastructure with a business data fabric.  In July 2024, the following updates gave business users stronger control over data views, resources, and import flexibility.

  • Compatibility contracts
    By setting a data view’s release state to “Released,” business users can help ensure output columns remain consistent until they’re replaced by a successor. This feature also enables gradual decommissioning while maintaining system integrity.

  • Workload management
    Resource management can be centralized by defining priorities and statement limits for all spaces. In return, business users can manage workload distribution effectively, while deprecating parameters for the command-line interface simplify updates in future releases.

  • Space changes during content import
    Specific spaces can now be chosen during imports. Space mapping added flexibility to support intra-tenant transport. Business users can import content into different spaces within the same tenant, even creating multiple copies in various spaces if needed.

🔖Learn more about SAP Datasphere's new features

SAP Analytics Cloud: Improving insight depth and speed

SAP Analytics Cloud, also part of SAP BTP, has been enhanced with several powerful updates that streamline data analysis, planning, and presentation. With these new features, SAP Analytics Cloud is adding even more depth and flexibility for customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making it easier for teams across the business to access insights and make data-driven decisions.

Here’s a look at some of the most impactful advancements:

  • Report-report interface
    Organizations with live connections to the SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) application can now leverage the report-report interface to analyze data in context. With this feature, users can jump from making queries in SAP BW—from generating ABAP reports and transactions to analyzing data.

  • Natural language queries
    Enabled by generative AI, the just ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud allows users to ask questions in their natural language and receive instant data-driven answers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud while reducing their dependence on IT support. Recent updates include tools to simplify the user experience, such as the prompt guide, model control options for administrators, and advanced comparisons.

  • Plan-data files uploading
    Starting with the Q3 2024 release, business users are now able to enhance their planning efficiency by uploading their plan-data files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly into SAP Analytics Cloud. They can initiate uploads through triggers in stories, and administrators can set up data structures and upload jobs to help ensure data consistency.

  • Standard unit conversion
    The standard unit conversion feature simplifies data handling by allowing modelers and planners to use predefined conversion tables for measurements like length and weight. This capability helps ensure consistent unit representation across reports and stories, supporting data accuracy in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud (part 2): Extending planning and analysis business-wide

This year’s updates also brought powerful enhancements for planning and analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud. The following innovations were added to SAP Analytics Cloud to further streamline workflows, foster collaboration, and support strategic insights across departments, particulary for users with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Triggering of calendar events from external applications
    Enhancing workflow automation for enterprise planning scenarios, this capability simplifies end-to-end planning processes and helps ensure key tasks are executed without disruption across integrated platforms.

  • Automated synchronization of calendar events
    With automatic synchronization of SAP calendar events with Microsoft Teams and Outlook, users can keep track of critical planning deadlines and events. This integration improves productivity and collaboration, embedding SAP planning directly into users’ daily calendars.

  • Industry-specific business content
    New and updated content packages in SAP Analytics Cloud provide prebuilt templates and analytics tools for business areas, such as sustainability, finance, sales, and the public sector. These packages accelerate the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, allowing companies to leverage industry-specific insights more quickly and effectively.

  • Probability simulation
    The compass functionality of SAP Analytics Cloud facilitates probability simulations that allow organizations to anticipate risks and model scenarios across key business drivers. Decision-makers can simulate the best, worst, and realistic outcomes—leading to more-proactive strategies to mitigate emerging disruptions.

  • Continuous planning integration
    The integration feature for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere forms a unified, governed data environment for planning and analytics. By centralizing data on one platform, this feature fosters collaboration across departments and reduces time-to-action for mission-critical decisions.

🔖Learn more about the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP HANA Cloud: Extending AI support and larger data volumes

 

SAP HANA Cloud is SAP’s database as a service solution, designed for large scale deployments.  The new features in SAP HANA Cloud for 2024 provide improved support for artificial intelligence and larger deployements, along with many quality of life features. Here are just a few of the highlights:

  • General availability of the Vector Engine for SAP HANA Cloud
    The introduction of the vector engine allows SAP HANA Cloud to provide the foundation for large language models, facilitating more contextually accurate responses to business queries. Learn more in this related blog post.

  • Support for larger instances
    With the 2024 Q2 release, the amount of memory available to allocate to a database instance increased to 12 TB. This allows better support for memory-intensive workloads.
  • Cloud Reliability – Backup Compression
    New backup compression for data, log, and catalog backups for SAP HANA database instances in SAP HANA Cloud reduces the amount of backup storage consumed, while ensuring the integrity and security of the backups.

  • Online migration
    The newly introduced online migration capabilities allow critical business processes to remain operational within the source SAP HANA system during the migration process. This innovation minimizes migration downtime, automates migration tasks, and reduces the need for manual interventions.

  • New machine learning options
    Multiple new features to the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) in SAP HANA Cloud facilitates faster modeling and application of AutoML, and automate analytics processes. The features allow a single neural network model to predict multiple related target columns. Other enhancements include the hierarchical density-based clustering of data with noise (HDBSCAN) and support for clustering of vector data using the DBSCAN and HDBSCAN functions.
    Learn more in the blog post provided by Christoph Morgen, as well as in this community post
  • Support for AWS PrivateLink services
    This new capability enables users to connect applications running within an Amazon Web Services (AWS) to SAP HANA Cloud using private IP endpoints, enhancing security. Learn more in this demo video.

Many more new features are described by @thomashammer in his blog post series.  

🔖 Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud

Closing

These new features in SAP BTP don't just make it easier to get the most from the data and processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud—they also build a solid foundation for growth and adaptability. When you use these tools together, you’re creating an ERP experience that’s ready for whatever comes next.

Learn more about how SAP BTP enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud at sap.com/btp-for-s4hana.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

As follow-up to our previous release 2408, this blog post provides an update concerning our most recent release 2411, which is available for customers and partners since November 16th. This particular release features highlights such as ABAP Cloud in SAP Build, a new approach to build Application Jobs, and the region-less subscription of the Landscape Portal.

The complete list of all new features can be found on the What´s New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment page.

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Creating an ABAP Cloud Project in the Context of the SAP BTP ABAP environment, a service key is no longer needed when creating an ABAP Cloud Project. You now enter the ABAP Service instance URL (details)
  • Triggering Where-Used List for Usages of CDS Annotation to enable the investigation in which ABAP CDS an annotation is used
  • Displaying Field Characteristics for RAP Types in the Element Information Popup, The Element Information popup (F2) for RAP types shows additional field characteristics information such as mandatory:create, mandatory:execute or readonly (details)
  • Multi-Dimensional Analysis App Generator, you can now use the multi-dimensional analysis app generator wizard to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a multi-dimensional analysis Fiori app (details)
  • Support of SAP Fiori launchpad app descriptor items in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these items describe how an application is started and consist of a target mapping and one or more tiles, for more information see SAP Fiori Applications in the ABAP Environment and Working with Launchpad App Descriptor Items
  • Rerunning Unit Tests with Coverage in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • Clearing Coverage results, you can now clear your coverage results in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • ABAP Test Cockpit: ATC Check "Usage of Released APIs" can be run on Key User and ABAP Cloud objects

Reuse Services

  • New Class Interface for Application Job Catalog Entries and Templates, with the new attribute-based class interface it is now possible to change the parameters, sections, and groups of a catalog entry (details)
  • Business Rule Framework (BRF plus) is enhanced with a code generation service and an API introduced to facilitate the consumption of business rules within ABAP Cloud (details)
  • XCO XSLX: You can now style the cells of an Excel worksheet. You can also modify existing documents (details)
  • XCO CDS: You can now generate CDS external entities and logical external schemas, and you can now add comments and arithmetic expressions to CDS views (details)

UI Services and SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Creating Launchpad App Descriptor Items (details)

Integration

  • With Business Event Logging, you can capture and log business events locally within the ABAP environment
  • App Display Business Event Logs. It provides an overview of logged business events for different objects and event types. You can explore the details of the event with this app
  • App Display Business Events by Objects: It groups business events by instances of objects and object components
  • App Display Changes to Objects: You can see the changes for your objects on a field level as recorded in business event logging

Security

  • ABAP environment tenants are now equipped with a signed default client certificate, which is automatically rotated and can be used for customer managed integration scenarios
  • The communication scenario SAP_COM_0915 provides access to APIs to read the Read Access Logs from the system, the API can for instance be leveraged by a Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) system
  • TLS 1.3, we now support Transport Layer Security 1.3 for outbound HTTP, WebSocket RFC, and SMTP protocols
  • The system exposes APIs to retrieve customer-managed security settings in a read-only manner

Custom Code Migration App

  • Include ADT links in SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check Export, the export of ATC results for the SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check now includes HTTP(S) links, which enables you to navigate to the source code of example code in ADT for Eclipse. This new functionality is available in the Custom Code Migration app only (details)
  • New option to start analysis after project creation, you can now choose whether you would like to start the custom code analysis run automatically at project creation or whether you would like to schedule it manually after project creation (details)
  • Changes in Scope Calculation Automation, you now need to initiate scope calculation manually after project creation, the scope calculation no longer starts automatically at project creation (details)

Cloud ATC

  • Change Validity Date for ATC Exemptions in the Approve ATC Exemptions App (details)
  • Replication of ATC Exemptions from on-premise towards SAP BTP, Customers need to use transaction SATC_EXPORT_XMPT to download the exemptions from an on-premise ATC system (details)

Technical Monitoring

  • Enhanced app HANA Table Analysis: With the new Column Loads and Column Unloads screens in the HANA Table Analysis app, you can check how many columns of the tables in your HANA database have been loaded to or unloaded from the main storage (details)
  • New app HANA Memory Analysis: With the new app, you can get an overview of the memory consumption of the HANA memory components over time. This helps you to identify bottlenecks in your HANA memory (details)

Lifecycle Management

  • When cloning a software component, you can now optionally use the newly added parameters to request cloning a Bring Your Own Git (BYOG) repository (details)

abapGit

  • Support of object deletions, it can now be exported from and imported to the ABAP environment with abapGit (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Extended Access, you can now access and subscribe to the Landscape Portal for SAP BTP ABAP Environment from every region that is available for the ABAP environment. In regions without SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, some applications in the Landscape Portal are limited or not available (details)
  • Deinstalling Products, the new Undeploy Product app in the Landscape Portal enables you to permanently deinstall existing products (details)

entry.title: #07 - Exploring Your SAP Solution: Engaging Stakeholders Early in the Implementation Process
entry.content:
Why is it important to familiarize yourself with your solution prior to implementation?

Let me share a story about a customer I worked with who took the time to enable their team before starting the project implementation and design discussions.

This customer was new to SAP and purchased a solution that was unfamiliar to them, yet critical for their business. As we recommend to all our customers, we advised them to undergo solution enablement before project implementation. This approach would help them become more familiar with the solution and maximize its value throughout the implementation process.

You’re probably wondering how it went for that customer. Well, I can tell you—it went really well!

When we began the design workshops, we presented the standard capabilities, as we always recommend to our customers for creating a scalable solution. However, we also recognize that business-specific requirements often call for adjustments. During these discussions, our customer was not only better prepared to engage with the topics and explanations we provided, but they were also able to identify key functionalities that would help enhance their business.

These design discussions are crucial during project implementation because they define the foundation of the customer’s solution. If the design is not done correctly from the start, the customer may end up with a solution that isn’t viable. Moreover, having the right solution design in place ensures that, during the testing phase, you're validating the solution effectively—rather than testing irrelevant processes that don’t align with the business needs. And that’s exactly what happened—my customer ended up with a solution that perfectly met their needs.

Another benefit was the time efficiency. By enabling themselves early on, the workshop discussions ran smoothly and stayed within the defined timelines, allowing us to progress without any delays. And it wasn’t just in the early stages where we were time-efficient; throughout all phases of the project implementation, we stayed on track because the scope of what needed to be delivered was clear from the start.

Finally, the entire team was engaged from day one and understood the impact of implementing a new solution. Typically, when a new solution is being implemented, not all users immediately embrace the change. Senior leadership is eager to move to a new system to improve business performance, increase margins and save costs, but business users are often resistant, especially when they are unfamiliar with the new system.

However, by enabling key stakeholders early on, we were able to make a significant impact on the skeptical business users. These stakeholders were able to champion the solution, sharing the added value it brought for the business users by streamlining their daily tasks. This empowered the business users to engage during the discussions and focus on higher-value activities, keeping them motivate. It also reinforced that everyone was contributing to the overall success of the business.

 

In a nutshell:

Gaining knowledge about SAP solution ahead of time is crucial, allows customers to maximize the value of the system from day one. 

By familiarizing themselves with the features, functionalities, and best practices before implementation, customers can ensure a smoother transition, reduce potential challenges, and align the solution more effectively with their business goals. This proactive approach allows teams to ask informed questions, make strategic decisions, and prepare their organization for a more seamless integration.

Furthermore, early exposure to the SAP solution empowers customers to take full advantage of its capabilities, ensuring they are ready to leverage its tools and insights to drive business success once the system goes live.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira  or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Output Management Solutions For LOBs In SAP Business Technology Platform To Keep Core Clean
entry.content:
In our LOBs systems like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA OnPremise, scope item Output Management (1LQ) will cover the requirements of rendering PDF form used by printing, email.
 
But there are scenarios where the requirements can not be realized with standard function of 1LQ,the following are some examples:
  • Missing data even tried with key user extensibility.
  • Missing functions like need digital signature in PDF form.
  • Customers need pull data from multiple systems to render PDF form in realtime.
  • Customers need a stand alone application for partners to print PDF form with data from LOB systems in realtime.
  • Customer need to render PDF form in mobile device with data from LOB systems in realtime.
  • Partners want to deploy SAAS solution for customers for Output Management.
  • Other scenarios.
Some scenarios can be realized with On-stack extensibility in ABAP environment. To keep core clean,cloud ABAP is recommended, classic technologies like smartform, SAPscript should be avoided. Adobe Forms technology is recommended.
 
To keep core clean, Side-By-Side extensibility in SAP Business Technology Platform(BTP) are highly recommended for loosely-coupled scenarios. For Output Management solutions in BTP, we have 2 styles solutions.
 
First style is automatically label printing or email. This means when business object like billing created in LOB system, the PDF form is automatically rendered with predefined format are automatically sent to a local printer or emailed to specific contact. I wrote a blog Build Digital Document Platform Backend Service On BTP with BTP Ads Sdm Print Service before. If we use the solution in this blog, we can realize automatically label printing with SAP Integration Suite or SAP Build Process Automation very efficiently. The following is a solution architecture for a real customer use case.

 
Second style is Side-By-Side extensibility with CAP or RAP as the backend, SAP UI5 as the front end.
Here will deploy a SAP UI5 application in SAP Build Work Zone or SAP BTP ABAP environment for the end users. The following is one of the architectures:

 

For this style, we prepared the following Discovery Center Missions for you:
  • If you prefer CAP in NodeJs, you can follow the mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js
  • If you prefer CAP in Java,you can follow the mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Java
  •  If you prefer RAP(Cloud ABAP),you can follow the mission Add Printing Label to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud In BTP ABAP Environment
All the 3 missions are base on best practice of Side-By-Side extensibility. You can apply the same method in other Side-By-Side extensibility scenarios.
 
The mission Add Printing Label to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is based on a little old technology. I will retire the mission.
 
To better help our customers to learn the solutions, we prepared the following webinars in December 2024. Welcome to register with the following linkages, we are happy to explain and answer queries and discuss.
 
Little remind: please reach out to your BTP Customer Success Manager to get the CRM ID for your organisation before the register.
  • 4TH December 2024, Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js
  • 11ST December 2024, Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Java
These 2 solutions are based on CAP. If you are interested in CAP,I think you can start with the Learning Sources.



The Side-By-Side extensibility method used in the missions can be applied to other LOBs like SAP ECC,SAP SuccessFactors HCM,etc.

 

The ends!
Thanks for your time!

 

Best regards!
Jacky Liu

entry.title: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP

    🧰👷‍Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects👷‍🧰

 

Back in 2013 when I was studying for SAP HANA Certification I published the following blog, as a central point of reference for SAP HANA technical information, it helped me with what I was studying and helped others with having piles of very useful information in one place:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/the-sap-hana-reference-for-sap-basis-administrators/ba-p/13007539

Fast forward 12 years, and we are now in the same situation with SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP, there is useful documentation all over the place and it would help if there was a central location for SAP Technology Architects to use as a "Toolbox". It will certainly help me 🙂

Implementing and Migrating to and Running S/4 PCE on RISE for the last two years, and as usual collecting a mountain of the most useful documentation as a personal toolbox, it's been clear to see that similar to the situation back in the earlier period of HANA, there is useful RISE documentation in a number of locations, this blog will act as a central resource pointing to all of the locations of the SAP S/4 PCE RISE documentation.

And similarly for the BTP, having been doing BTP (or as it was the SAP Cloud Platform) for 7 years, this blog will be a central location pointing to the most useful BTP documentation for SAP Technology Architects.

The two subjects are combined, SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE with BTP, because in my eyes the two go hand in hand, S/4HANA PCE on RISE is the next generation of the previous On-Premise ERP offerings from SAP, and the BTP contains the next generation of Applications which we will all use with the S/4HANA PCE on RISE, including for example, and not limited to, Integration Suite and CPI & EM/AEM & APIM, obviously CALM as this is replacing in many but not all cases what we do with SolMan, and all of the other Services which we will need pretty much day by day to run S/4HANA PCE on RISE, and of course, Steampunk, keeping the Core Clean, and "Upgrade Stable" (as my friend Sakthi would say), then we need the ABAP Service in the BTP for the side car extensions. But that's just the BTP "Starter Pack", as you go on the BTP journey you will be using all kinds of Services and including the AI Joule and the Business Application Studio and the Work Zone, Custom Domain, ADS, Secure Logon, CTMS  etc.

If you have links to useful documentation which you think will help others and should be added to the list, then please just write it in the comments and I will add it to the list.

Please expect to find changes and new information here, because it will be a live work in progress, it's going to take a few weeks to list and classify everything that I already have.

Tip: search the list for what you are looking for, eg FUE Licensing details

This is a good one from Jarod Sandham, some useful search criteria to find documentation is to search "https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/ <insert search criteria here>"

So open google and search for:  https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/  security

 

Here we go....

You might be asking yourself why in a SAP RISE Toolbox there are links to how to do a SAP CCOE ?

That's a great question, and the answer is that, most companies have been running/operating SAP the same way since 3.1h, and now with RISE the whole game is changing, the Roles and Responsibilities are changing and the Segregation of Duties is changing, between who does what, SAP, Customer, and if they have one the Customer's System Integrator. 

And that's why, during the S/4HANA RISE Transformation, you will be best off, if you take the opportunity and redesign how you run/operate SAP.

Andy Silvey.

SAP Technology Architect [Contractor]

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto  inc, an startup which is making Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto dot io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto dot io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

All of this makes atkrypto dot io the DePIN Decentralised Physical Infrastructure Network solution for Enterprise.

entry.title: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Migration Guide
entry.content:
This service migration guide provides a 6-step procedure with which you can smoothly setup, test, and eventually handover logging from Application Logging to Cloud Logging service.

It offers a side-by-side setup of Cloud Logging, lets data flow simultaneously into both, Application & Cloud Logging, and once you are satisfied, you can switch your observability procedures onto Cloud Logging and shutdown Application Logging.

It's complemented by a service innovation guide which helps to understand the full potential of Cloud Logging and where it differs from Application Logging.

1) Plan your Cloud Logging setup

Based on the knowledge from service Innovation guide, first plan the following:

  • How many instances?
  • How to centralize?
  • Size of each instance?
  • Who should have access to which instances?

2) Setup Identity Provider

In Cloud Logging, you have to configure your own user management (IDP). To have the same Single Sign On (SSO) experience, you must bring your own IAS account. Please refer to this guide on how to create a tenant SAML application in SAP BTP’S Identity Authentication Service (IAS).

  • SAML authentication cannot be enabled on an existing Cloud Logging Service instance with a service update. Parameters must be passed on service creation.
  • If you decide to utilize multiple CLS instances, you could use leverage the same SAML application from IAS to setup all CLS instances.

Note: Omitting the SAML configuration would fall back to Basic Authentication, which could be useful for evaluation purposes.

3) Setup Cloud Logging

Create instances of Cloud Logging Service and bind your applications based on our documentation.

  • Enter your SAML configuration as a configuration parameter derived from the previous step. You cannot enable SAML authentication on an existing Cloud Logging Service instance with a service update.
  • You have more freedom to configure, compared to Application Logging. For example, you can configure up to 90 days of retention. See Configuration Parameters to have a suitable parametrization.

4) Bind and instrument your applications

Bind your application to Cloud Logging – in parallel to the existing binding to Application Logging.

This let’s log data flow simultaneously into both, Application & Cloud Logging, so gives a smooth transition and allows to compare the results before the eventual switch.

Note: Since there is not ingestion-based quota in Cloud Logging, you might notice an increased ingestion as all logs are ingested. Application logging Statistics dashboard provides insights into the quota decisions. For each application, the aggregated message count and volume of accepted and rejected messages is reported for short time intervals. This allows determination of the total message volume, enabling an informed decision on the required service plan.

In case you use custom fields, please refer to the Annex B for instrumentation guide.

5) Validate Setup

Check if logs arrive and are parsed correctly. Remember, you have your own OpenSearch Dashboards, and your own URL which can be read from the cf service-key and that contains all endpoints and credentials.

To learn what you can do, check the using your Cloud Logging Service instance documentation and see how to ingest, alert, manage access rights, and so on.

6) Deprovision Application Logging

After at most 7 days, Cloud Logging instances hold all the logs which are in Application Logging.

So, you can unbind the applications from Application Logging instances (cf unbind-service) and delete the Application Logging service instance (cf delete-service). Existing bindings must be deleted before the instance can be deprovisioned.

 

Annex A) Process Visualization

The following visualizations explain the 6-step migration process via a concrete example on how the respective setups of application logging and cloud logging may look like.

 

Annex B) Adjust Custom Fields Configuration When Sending with Logging Libraries

This section applies only if you are sending custom fields with one of our logging libraries (Java/NodeJS). Application Logging requires configuring custom fields in the logging library, while Cloud Logging Service supports them without additional configuration.

The migration requires a configuration change to support both services simultaneously. You must send custom fields in #cf nested object and at top-level.

Node.js: The CF NodeJS logging library automatically detects which services you are bound to (Application Logging and/or Cloud Logging Service) and adjusts sending custom fields since version v6.5.0. Make sure you updated the logging library to this version or higher.

Java: You must configure the CF Java logging library, dependent on the logging backend you use.

  • For Log4j Logging Backend: Set retainOriginal to true in your log4j XML configuration file. That means replacing <customField mdcKeyName="custom-field" retainOriginal="false" /> with <customField mdcKeyName="custom-field" retainOriginal="true" /> for all custom fields.
  • For Logback Logging Backend: For each custom-field configuration line, another one has to be appended to retain that custom field at top level. For example, the <customFieldMdcKeyName>custom-field</customFieldMdcKeyName> configuration line should be followed by this one: <retainFieldMdcKeyName>custom-field</retainFieldMdcKeyName>.

After deprovisioning the Application Logging instance, you can remove all configuration regarding custom fields.

entry.title: Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance

We are excited to announce the release of the new version of the Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance (MDG), which has recently been published on Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

This updated version includes new solution variants for SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 in classic mode and in cloud-ready mode. One of the key highlights of this release is the inclusion of detailed diagrams for business partner, product, and financial domain for master data governance in classic mode. These diagrams provide a comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, making it easier for users to understand how MDG fits into their existing IT landscape and how it can be integrated with other systems and applications.  

Updates in Detail

1. Solution Variant “Federated Master Data Governance with Core Data Owner on SAP S/4HANA”

We added the information that Federated Master Data Governance can also be used with MDG on lower releases/classic-mode. More information can be found in the how-to guide and in the blog "Consumption of Federated Governance Made Easy" from @Mark63.

In the next two solution variants, we have replaced the existing one for “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA” with two new solution variants to illustrate the supported processes: 

2. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Classic Mode”

In the Solution Value Flow Diagram, you can see that MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode supports multiple domains, including business partner, product, and financials. This diagram provides a clear representation of how a business process variant is implemented and supported by a specific solution. 

As example you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners. In the case of Decentral Creation, a consolidation process is used, whereas for Decentral Change, the MDG, Central Governance Change Request is used. 

The descriptive text next to the diagram contains more information: It provides details about the standard delivered process template and change request types that are suitable for decentralized creation and change processes.  

3. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Cloud-Ready Mode”

In comparison to the Solution Variant for MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode you can see the Solution Value Flow Diagram for cloud-ready mode currently only contains the Business Partner Domain. 

The Solution Process Flow Diagrams were initially published last year. We have now included three new diagrams, which demonstrate the support for Mass Load, Mass Change, and Mass Deletion of Business Partner Relationships. 

Furthermore, to draw a comparison with classic mode diagrams, here you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners in cloud-ready mode. In both cases, consolidation processes are used. 

We believe that this latest release will be a valuable resource for organizations looking to implement or expand their MDM capabilities. By providing a clear and comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, we aim to empower users to make the most of their MDM investment and to achieve their data management goals more efficiently.  

We invite you to explore the new Reference Solution Architecture for MDM by yourself on the Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

Further information

Curious about the initially released content and about the different diagram types such as Solution Value Flow diagram, Solution Component diagram, Solution Data Flow diagram, Solution Process Flow diagram? 

  • Check the blog post “Explore Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance” from my colleague @KarinBlank. 
  • Watch our Video: How to use Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance - YouTube 

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, join the Questions & Answers forum and Blog space, and check the MDG roadmap for all MDG versions. 

Best, Christina Lindner

entry.title: SAP Developer News, November 21st 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: SAP Developer News November 21st, 2024 - SAP Developers

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2411/ba-p/13940790

SAP BTP Innobytes October 2024 edition

  • Video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9xZCTtSGP_0

SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 and SAP Analytics Cloud 2024/Q4

  • SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 Released: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-2-0-sps-08-released-safeguarding-customer-investments-and/ba-p/13942543
  • What’s New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 sessions: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-2-0-sps-08/ba-p/13941640
  • Calculation View Features of SAP HANA 2.0 SPS08: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/calculation-view-features-of-sap-hana-2-0-sps08/ba-p/13927860
  • What's New Viewer - SAP Analytics Cloud: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/42e4f84a0e5e458792b1047eaf81c31a?locale=en-US
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Q4 2024 Release's Features with Experts & Demos: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=atNQxqkf0Z8&list=PL3ZRUb1AKkpTv44lv8pQvdlwT_MzAi3Qq&ab_channel=SAP

SAP Open Source Webinar on the OpenSearch Foundation

  • Details and sign-up https://events.sap.com/opensearch-foundation/en/registration.aspx

SAP Berlin Event

  • Info and to register: https://events.sap.com/eur/sapbuild-and-agilita-workshop/en/home

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:07 SAP TechEd On Tour from SAUG-x

1:04 SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

2:07 SAP BTP Innobytes October 2024 edition

3:19 SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 and SAP Analytics Cloud 2024/Q4

5:02 SAP Berlin Event

6:16 SAP Open Source Webinar on the OpenSearch Foundation

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for November 21st, 2024.

[Josh and Michelle] Hey everybody, this is Josh and Michelle, and we are live from Melbourne, Australia here at the SAUGx event part of the TechEd on tour. I had a great time being here not only taking part in keynote we are standing in the Developer Garage which you may recognize from other TechEd events. We did this in Florida at ASUG, we are doing here at SAUGx and I believe there's two more events coming up right Michelle? Yes, we have one in UKI and one in Germany a little bit later next year. The DSAG event is like in April, I think. Yeah. Yeah. So, we're on tour. We're coming after you guys. We hope that you enjoy meeting with us, the developer advocates in person. Lots of great things to cover from what was announced at TechEd, as well as lots of great tutorials you can complete for some great little prizes that we have here in the Developer Garage. Oh, absolutely. And they've been flying off as soon as people have been finishing so many tutorial tracks to get them all. Alright, so that's it from Australia. We'll see you guys later.

[Rich Heilman] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here coming to you live from SAUGx in Melbourne, Australia. I wanted to take a quick minute and let you know about the new release of SAP BTP ABAP environment 2411 and of course the new blog post from Nora Klemp of ABAP product management. Nora gives us all the details about the new features coming in release 2411 including items like, you can now use Multidimensional Analysis App Generator, Wizard, to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a Multidimensional Analysis Fiori application. So that's cool right? We're also getting some enhancements to BRF Plus and the XCO libraries, a ton of enhancements around business events, and much much more. For more information on the BTP ABAP environment released 24-11, check out Noor's blog post today. See you later. I'm gonna go jump on a plane. Bye for now, friends.

[DJ] Just in case you missed it, the October 2024 edition of SAP BTP InnoBytes is out. And it's all out AI and also Terraform. So on the AI front, there's a new SAP Business AI feature catalog on the SAP Discovery Center. And alongside the feature catalog, there's also a feature estimator with which you can work out costs and savings. And there's also a new generative AI based content generation assistant on the SAP Automation Pilot platform. And for setup of mission-related infrastructure on BTP, you can now use Terraform, which in contrast to the original booster-based setup mechanisms, allows you to download the Terraform scripts to use. And also, of course, that means you can adopt them and extend them for use in your own landscapes and environments. Open and free. Infrastructure as code. What's not to like? more about all these new features in this October 2024's edition of the SAP BTP InnoBytes video. Link in the description.

[Witalij] SAP HANA database powers over 70,000 customers worldwide, supporting some of the most critical applications and workloads. With the general availability of SAP HANA 2.0 SPS08 as of November 20, we at SAP are delivering key updates to enhance scalability, reliability, and developer experience while reducing the total cost of ownership. This release is designed to protect customer investments in our leading multi-model translitical database platform. Starting Friday, November 22, tune into SAP Developer's channel for our What's New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 series, hosted by truly yours, Developer Advocates. Over seven sessions, you'll hear from more than a dozen experts from the SAP HANA product management team. Each session will be packed with insights into the latest features and updates, along with guidance to help you make the most of them. Check out the full schedule in the blog post linked below. Don't miss out and join us. The QRC 2024 release of SAP Analytics Cloud went GA this week as well. You can explore the full list of all new features and enhancements in the What's New viewer on the SAP help portal. The SAP Analytics Cloud team has already released a video showcasing all the significant updates, complete with demos presented by product experts. Discover the highlights of the Q4 2024 release including bulk migration from classic stories to optimized experience, analytics catalog and grouping assets, story extensibility enhancements and so much more. Check it out today and start using these new features in in your tenant now.

[Daniel] I remember being invited to the first SAP Build Berlin event. It was called something like Burgers Beyond Budgets or something like that. Anyway, it was a great event. I got to talk a little bit. Everybody did the hands-on. And now there's a second event. Not that I was invited to it, but it's still going to be a great event.
